steps.title: Apply the intercept theorem for parallel lines
steps.content: Since $\overline{LM} \parallel \overline{PQ}$ and lines LQ and MP intersect at R, the intercept theorem gives us: $$\frac{LR}{RQ} = \frac{MR}{RP}$$ Substituting the given values: $$\frac{10}{RQ} = \frac{9}{19}$$ Cross multiply: $10 \times 19 = 9 \times RQ$, so $RQ = \frac{190}{9}$

steps.title: Calculate the total length LQ
steps.content: Since R lies on segment LQ between L and Q: $$LQ = LR + RQ = 10 + \frac{190}{9}$$ Convert 10 to ninths: $$LQ = \frac{90}{9} + \frac{190}{9} = \frac{280}{9}$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
